note: Shared glossary for all Cantar de mio Cid texts. Old Spanish forms are glossed with their dictionary form (or modern Spanish equivalent) and a terse meaning; = introduces the modern Spanish spelling.

glossary.sos: so — his, her, their (= modern sus)
glossary.tornava: tornar — he/she turned (imperfect); tornar = to turn, return
glossary.cabeça: cabeça — head (= cabeza; ç sounded ts)
glossary.i: e/i — and (variant of e before some sounds)
glossary.estávalos: estar + los — he-was + them: 'was … them' (estava + enclitic los)
glossary.catando: catar — looking at, beholding (gerund; archaic for mirar)
glossary.vío: veer — he saw (strong preterite of veer 'to see'; = vio, here two syllables ví-o)
glossary.abiertas: abierto — open (fem. pl.; past part. of abrir)
glossary.alcándaras: alcándara — perch, rack (for clothes or hunting birds; Arabic loan)
glossary.pielles: piel — fur, pelt, fur garment (pl. pielles = pieles)
glossary.mudados: mudado — moulted (of a hawk: having shed feathers in captivity, i.e. trained and valuable)
glossary.mio: mio — my (unstressed possessive; mio Çid 'my lord Cid')
glossary.çid: Çid — the Cid, 'lord' (Arabic sīdī 'my lord'; pronounced Tsid)
glossary.avié: aver — he had (imperfect of aver = haber, here a full verb 'to have, hold')
glossary.fabló: fablar — he spoke (preterite; = habló, note f-)
glossary.mesurado: mesurado — measured, restrained, with mesura (courtly self-command)
glossary.tí: tú — thee (stressed object pronoun; = ti, old accentuation)
glossary.an: aver — they have (3rd pl. present of aver = han)
glossary.buolto: bolver — stirred up, brewed, plotted (past part. of bolver = volver; bolver traición 'to weave treachery')
glossary.pienssan: pensar — they think; pensar de + inf. 'to set about (doing)' (epic formula)
glossary.aguijar: aguijar — to spur (one's horse), ride hard
glossary.las: el/la — the (fem. pl.); them (fem. object pronoun)
glossary.exida: exida — going out, departure (= salida; Latin exire)
glossary.bivar: Bivar — Vivar, the Cid's home village just north of Burgos
glossary.ovieron: aver — they had, they got (strong preterite of aver; = hubieron)
glossary.diestra: diestro — right, on the right side (fem.)
glossary.burgos: Burgos — Burgos, chief city of Old Castile
glossary.siniestra: siniestro — left, on the left side (fem.; ill-omened)
glossary.meçió: meçer — he shook (preterite of meçer 'to shake'; = meció)
glossary.engrameó: engramear — he tossed, shook (the head) — rare epic verb
glossary.tiesta: tiesta — head (cf. French tête; Latin testa)
glossary.albricia: albricia — good tidings! reward-for-good-news (Arabic al-bishāra); here a defiant cry
glossary.alvar: Alvar — Álvar (Fáñez), the Cid's kinsman and lieutenant ('Minaya')
glossary.echados: echar — cast out, thrown (past part. pl.); echados somos 'we are banished'
glossary.somos: seer — we are (1st pl. of seer = ser)
glossary.tierra: tierra — land, country; one's own land
glossary.roy: Roy — Ruy, short for Rodrigo (Roy Díaz = Rodrigo Díaz, the Cid)
glossary.entróve: entrar — he entered (entró + archaic paragogic -e restored for the assonance)
glossary.pendones: pendón — pennon, lance-banner (men are counted by lances)
glossary.exien: exir — they went out (imperfect of exir 'to go out'; = salían)
glossary.veer: veer — to see (= ver; two syllables)
glossary.plorando: plorar — weeping (gerund; older pl- form of llorar — the poem uses both)
glossary.dizían: dezir — they were saying (imperfect of dezir = decir)
glossary.si: si — if; si + subjunctive alone = 'if only …!'
glossary.oviesse: aver — he had, he might have (imperfect subjunctive of aver; = hubiese)
glossary.conbidar: conbidar — to invite, take in as guest (= convidar)
glossary.ien: ien — they would (auxiliary of the split conditional: conbidar le ien = le convidarían; from avién)
glossary.alfonsso: Alfonsso — Alfonso (VI, king of León and Castile)
glossary.avie: aver — he had (imperfect; unaccented spelling beside avié)
glossary.dél: de + él — from him, of him (contraction)
glossary.recabdo: recabdo — care, precaution, strict order (= recaudo)
glossary.seellada: seellar — sealed (past part. fem.; seello < Latin sigillum, modern sello)
glossary.diessen: dar — they should give (imperfect subjunctive pl.; = diesen)
glossary.gela: ge + la — it to-him (ge = old dative 'to him/her', later se: modern se la)
glossary.diesse: dar — he should give (imperfect subjunctive; = diese)
glossary.sopiesse: saber — he should know (imperfect subjunctive of saber; = supiese)
glossary.vera: vero — true (fem.; vera palabra 'true word, sure truth')
glossary.averes: aver — goods, possessions, wealth (aver as noun, pl.)
glossary.yentes: yente — people, folk (= gente; y- from Latin g-)
glossary.cristianas: cristiano — Christian (fem. pl.; las yentes cristianas = 'everyone')
glossary.ascóndense: asconder + se — they hide themselves (asconden + enclitic se; = esconderse)
glossary.nada: nada — nothing; por nada 'for anything (in the world)'
glossary.campeador: Campeador — the Battler, the Champion (the Cid's epithet, from campo 'field of battle')
glossary.adeliñó: adeliñar — he made straight for, headed to (preterite)
glossary.así: así — thus, so; así como 'as soon as'
glossary.puorta: puorta — door (older uo-diphthong spelling of puerta)
glossary.fallóla: fallar + la — he-found-it (falló + enclitic la; fallar = hallar, note f-)
glossary.pararan: parar — they had arranged, agreed (pluperfect in -ra; parar 'to arrange, settle')
glossary.quebrantás: quebrantar — he should break (apocopated imperfect subjunctive = quebrantasse)
glossary.abriessen: abrir — they should open (imperfect subjunctive pl.; = abriesen)
glossary.vozes: voz — voice (pl.; a altas vozes 'at the top of their voices')
glossary.querién: querer — they wished, would (imperfect 3rd pl.; = querían)
glossary.tornar: tornar — to return; tornar palabra 'to answer a word'
glossary.llegaua: llegar — he drew near (imperfect; copyist's u-for-v spelling of llegava)
glossary.ferídal: ferida + le — a blow to-it (ferida 'blow, stroke', = herida, note f-; + enclitic l(e))
glossary.era: seer — it was (imperfect of seer = ser)
glossary.ojo: ojo — eye; a ojo 'in plain sight'
glossary.parava: parar — she placed, stationed (imperfect; se parava 'she stood forth')
glossary.ya: ya — O! (vocative particle, Arabic yā); also 'now, already'
glossary.buena: bueno — good (fem.); en buen(a) '(born/girded) in a good hour' — the poem's honorific formula
glossary.çinxiestes: çeñir — you girded (strong preterite 2nd pl. of çeñir; Latin cinxistis)
glossary.ha: aver — he has (3rd sg. present; = ha)
glossary.osariemos: osar — we would dare (conditional 1st pl.; = osaríamos)
glossary.coger: coger — to take in, shelter (here = acoger)
glossary.perderiemos: perder — we would lose (conditional 1st pl.; = perderíamos)
glossary.casa: casa — house; also settlement, town (Burgos la casa 'the town of Burgos')
glossary.ganades: ganar — you gain (2nd pl. present; = ganáis)
glossary.vala: valer — may he avail, protect (present subjunctive; vos vala 'may he keep you')
glossary.vertudes: vertud — power, virtue (= virtud; sus vertudes santas 'his holy powers')
glossary.dixo: dezir — he/she said (strong preterite; x = sh-sound; = dijo)
glossary.tornós: tornar + se — she turned back (tornó + -s(e): after a stressed vowel -se shrinks to -s)
glossary.vede: veer — he sees (older present vede, Latin videt; MS reads vee)
glossary.partiós: partir + se — he departed (partió + -s(e); partirse 'to take one's leave')
glossary.dela: de + la — from the (copyist's run-together spelling)
glossary.aguijaua: aguijar — he spurred (imperfect; copyist's u-for-v spelling of aguijava)
glossary.santa: santo — Saint (fem.); Santa María, the cathedral of Burgos
glossary.maría: María — Mary; Santa María, the cathedral of Burgos
glossary.fincó: fincar — he planted, set firm (preterite; fincar los inojos 'to kneel')
glossary.inojos: inojo — knee (= hinojo; fincar los inojos 'to kneel')
glossary.fecha: fazer — made, done (past part. fem. of fazer = hacer; = hecha, note f-)
glossary.arlançón: Arlançón — the Arlanzón, the river of Burgos
glossary.cabo: cabo — beside, next to (preposition; cf. modern cabe)
glossary.çinxo: çeñir — he girded (strong preterite 3rd sg.; Latin cinxit)
glossary.commo: commo — as, like (spelling variant of como)
glossary.fosse: seer — he were (imperfect subjunctive of seer; MS fuesse, Pidal's older form)
glossary.l: le — to him (elided before vowel: l'an = le han)
glossary.quantas: quanto — as many as (fem. pl.; = cuantas)
glossary.osarien: osar — they would dare (conditional 3rd pl.; = osarían)
glossary.al: a + el — to the; al menos 'even the least'
glossary.dinarada: dinarada — a penny's-worth (what one dinero buys)
